text_original: Kun (represents) what is great and originating, penetrating, advantageous, correct and having the firmness of a mare. When the superior man (here intended) has to make any movement, if he take the initiative, he will go astray; if he follow, he will find his (proper) lord. The advantageousness will be seen in his getting friends in the south-west, and losing friends in the north-east. If he rest in correctness and firmness, there will be good fortune.
text_normalized: Kun (represents) what is great and originating, penetrating, advantageous, correct and having the firmness of a mare. When the superior man (here intended) has to make any movement, if he take the initiative, he will go astray; if he follow, he will find his (proper) lord. The advantageousness will be seen in his getting friends in the south-west, and losing friends in the north-east. If he rest in correctness and firmness, there will be good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=Judgment.

text_original: In the first SIX, divided, (we see its subject) treading on hoarfrost. The strong ice will come (by and by).
text_normalized: In the first SIX, divided, (we see its subject) treading on hoarfrost. The strong ice will come (by and by).
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=first.

text_original: The second SIX, divided, (shows the attribute of) being straight, square, and great. (Its operation), without repeated efforts, will be in every respect advantageous.
text_normalized: The second SIX, divided, (shows the attribute of) being straight, square, and great. (Its operation), without repeated efforts, will be in every respect advantageous.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=second.

text_original: The third SIX, divided, (shows its subject) keeping his excellence under restraint, but firmly maintaining it. If he should have occasion to engage in the king's service, though he will not claim the success (for himself), he will bring affairs to a good issue.
text_normalized: The third SIX, divided, (shows its subject) keeping his excellence under restraint, but firmly maintaining it. If he should have occasion to engage in the king's service, though he will not claim the success (for himself), he will bring affairs to a good issue.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=third.

text_original: The fourth SIX, divided, (shows the symbol of) a sack tied up. There will be no ground for blame or for praise.
text_normalized: The fourth SIX, divided, (shows the symbol of) a sack tied up. There will be no ground for blame or for praise.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fourth.

text_original: The fifth SIX, divided, (shows) the yellow lower garment. There will be great good fortune.
text_normalized: The fifth SIX, divided, (shows) the yellow lower garment. There will be great good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fifth.

text_original: The sixth SIX, divided (shows) dragons fighting in the wild. Their blood is purple and yellow.
text_normalized: The sixth SIX, divided (shows) dragons fighting in the wild. Their blood is purple and yellow.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=top.

text_original: (The lines of this hexagram are all weak and divided, as appears from) the use of the number six. If those (who are thus represented) be perpetually correct and firm, advantage will arise.
text_normalized: (The lines of this hexagram are all weak and divided, as appears from) the use of the number six. If those (who are thus represented) be perpetually correct and firm, advantage will arise.
